title: Monthly Status Report
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text:
Overall actual progress reached 34.2% against planned 35.5% . Status is now Amber/Red due to widening schedule drag at Cheras North Station , compounded by heavy rainfall, utility conflict escalation, and slower-than-planned precast segment output.
The package remains recoverable, but current trend indicates material schedule pressure unless three actions are completed in March:
1. utility authority approval is escalated and secured
2. alternate precast capacity is activated
3. phased interface approvals are accepted by the client team
contextualized_text:
Document: Monthly Status Report Feb 2026.pdf

Title: Monthly Status Report
Section: 1. Executive Summary
Page span: 1
Chunk type: mixed

1. Executive Summary
Overall actual progress reached 34.2% against planned 35.5% . Status is now Amber/Red due to widening schedule drag at Cheras North Station , compounded by heavy rainfall, utility conflict escalation, and slower-than-planned precast segment output.
The package remains recoverable, but current trend indicates material schedule pressure unless three actions are completed in March:
1. utility authority approval is escalated and secured
2. alternate precast capacity is activated
3. phased interface approvals are accepted by the client team

title: Monthly Status Report
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text: Schedule, Status = Red. Schedule, Commentary = Slippage widened in February, especially at Cheras North and utilities. Cost, Status = Amber. Cost, Commentary = Forecast moved upward due to recovery measures and VO growth. Safety, Status = Green. Safety, Commentary = No LTI; one near-miss follow-up remains active. Quality, Status = Amber. Quality, Commentary = Major concrete quality issues avoided, but one NCR closure still awaiting final verification. Stakeholders, Status = Amber. Stakeholders, Commentary = Utility and community constraints are restricting productivity windows

title: Monthly Status Report
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text: Persistent rainfall during February reduced productive time for pier erection and constrained concrete operations at open work fronts. The team resequenced several activities, but the lost productivity was only partially recovered.
contextualized_text:
Document: Monthly Status Report Feb 2026.pdf
Document type: progress_update
Reporting period: 2026-02
Project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
Title: Monthly Status Report
Section: 4.1 Weather Impact
Page span: 2
Chunk type: text

4.1 Weather Impact
Persistent rainfall during February reduced productive time for pier erection and constrained concrete operations at open work fronts. The team resequenced several activities, but the lost productivity was only partially recovered.

title: Monthly Status Report
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text: Additional underground services were identified in the Cheras North corridor, requiring redesign of the immediate work sequence and closer coordination with utility owners. The site team expects diversion completion in March, subject to authority clearance.
contextualized_text:
Document: Monthly Status Report Feb 2026.pdf
Document type: progress_update
Reporting period: 2026-02
Project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
Title: Monthly Status Report
Section: 4.2 Cheras North Utility Conflict
Page span: 2
Chunk type: text

4.2 Cheras North Utility Conflict
Additional underground services were identified in the Cheras North corridor, requiring redesign of the immediate work sequence and closer coordination with utility owners. The site team expects diversion completion in March, subject to authority clearance.

title: Monthly Status Report
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text: The precast segment supplier reported a temporary reduction in weekly output caused by mould maintenance and labour shortage. This has not yet become a critical path failure, but it is now a watch item.

4.3 Supply Chain
The precast segment supplier reported a temporary reduction in weekly output caused by mould maintenance and labour shortage. This has not yet become a critical path failure, but it is now a watch item.

title: Monthly Status Report
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text:
- Weekend and selected night concrete pours approved for critical viaduct fronts.
- Alternate span erection sequence developed to reduce weather exposure.
- Revised authority submission issued for telecom and water diversion crossing.
- Interface war room started with civil, systems, and employer representatives.

5. Recovery Actions Started
- Weekend and selected night concrete pours approved for critical viaduct fronts.
- Alternate span erection sequence developed to reduce weather exposure.
- Revised authority submission issued for telecom and water diversion crossing.
- Interface war room started with civil, systems, and employer representatives.
